en.extract:
The yellow-footed green pigeon (Treron phoenicopterus), also known as yellow-legged green pigeon, is a common species of green pigeon found in the Indian subcontinent and parts of Southeast Asia. It is the state bird of Maharashtra. In Marathi, it is called Haroli or Hariyal. It is known as Haitha in Upper Assam and Haitol in Lower Assam. The species feeds on fruit, including many species of Ficus. They forage in flocks. They are habitat generalists: in the early morning, they are often seen sunning on the tops of emergent trees in dense forest areas, especially Banyan trees, but they have also been spotted in natural remnants in urban areas. Their population is currently increasing.  


Range
This species is extant in Pakistan, India, Nepal, Sri Lanka, Bangladesh, Bhutan, Myanmar, China, Thailand, Vietnam, Laos and Cambodia.


References

Rasmussen, P. C. and Anderton, J. C. (2005) Birds of South Asia. The Ripley Guide. Vol 1 and 2. Smithsonian Institution and Lynx Editions.

[[File:Yellow footed green pegion.JPG|thumb|right|At Punjab Bhavan, [[New Delhi]].]]

The '''yellow-footed green pigeon''' ('''''Treron phoenicopterus'''''), also known as '''yellow-legged green pigeon''', is a common [[species]] of [[green pigeon]] found in the [[Indian subcontinent]] and parts of [[Southeast Asia]].<ref name=BLDZ/> It is the [[List of Indian state birds|state bird]] of [[Maharashtra]].<ref name=gn>{{cite news|url=http://www.governancenow.com/gov-next/green-gov/yellow-footed-green-pigeon-remain-maharashtra-state-bird |title=Yellow-footed green pigeon to remain Maharashtra state bird|work= GovernanceNow.com |date=June 28, 2011}}</ref><ref name=reb>{{cite news|author=Rebello, S. |url=http://www.hindustantimes.com/India-news/Mumbai/Yellow-footed-green-pigeon-retains-the-state-bird-tag/Article1-715024.aspx|url-status=dead|archive-date=27 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140527220120/http://www.hindustantimes.com/India-news/Mumbai/Yellow-footed-green-pigeon-retains-the-state-bird-tag/Article1-715024.aspx |title=Yellow-footed green pigeon retains the state bird tag|work=Hindustan Times|date= June 29, 2011}}</ref> In [[Marathi language|Marathi]], it is called ''Haroli'' or ''Hariyal''. It is known as ''Haitha'' in [[Upper Assam division|Upper Assam]] and ''Haitol'' in [[Lower Assam]]. The species feeds on fruit, including many species of ''[[Ficus]]''. They forage in flocks. They are habitat generalists:<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last1=Choudaj |first1=Kiran |last2=Shaha |first2=Chaitali |date=2023-06-01 |title=Natural remnants are refuges for rare birds in an urban area: a study from Pune city, India |journal=Ornis Hungarica |volume=31 |issue=1 |pages=62–71 |doi=10.2478/orhu-2023-0004 |issn=2061-9588|doi-access=free }}</ref> in the early morning, they are often seen [[Sunning (behaviour)|sunning]] on the tops of [[Rainforest#Emergent layer|emergent]] trees in dense forest areas, especially [[Banyan|Banyan trees]],<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Win |first=Nwet Nwet |display-authors=etal |date=June 2016 |title=ChemInform Abstract: Isopimarane Diterpenoids from ''Kaempferia pulchra'' Rhizomes Collected in Myanmar and Their Vpr Inhibitory Activity. |journal=ChemInform |volume=47 |issue=29 |article-number=chin.201629225 |doi=10.1002/chin.201629225 |issn=0931-7597}}</ref> but they have also been spotted in natural remnants in [[Urban area|urban]] areas.<ref name=":0" /> Their population is currently increasing.<ref name=":0" />  

== Range ==
This species is extant in [[Pakistan]], [[India]], [[Nepal]], [[Sri Lanka]], [[Bangladesh]], [[Bhutan]], [[Myanmar]], [[China]], [[Thailand]], [[Vietnam]], [[Laos]] and [[Cambodia]].<ref name=BLDZ>{{Cite web |title=Yellow-footed Green-pigeon ''Treron phoenicopterus'' Species Factsheet |url=https://datazone.birdlife.org/species/factsheet/yellow-footed-green-pigeon-treron-phoenicopterus |access-date=2025-11-23 |website=BirdLife DataZone |language=en}}</ref>

zh.extract:
黄脚绿鸠（学名：Treron phoenicopterus）为鸠鸽科绿鸠属的鸟类，俗名普通绿鸠。分布于印度、斯里兰卡、不丹、孟加拉、中南半岛以及中国大陆的云南等地，多栖息于丘陵及平原地带的森林及薮丛间以及多见于榕果或其他常吃野果丰富的树上。该物种的模式产地在印度。


保护
中国国家重点保护动物等级：二级


亚种
黄脚绿鸠云南亚种（学名：Treron phoenicopterus viridifrons）。分布于缅甸以及中国大陆的云南等地。该物种的模式产地在缅甸丹那沙林。
